item.title:
DIMON SOUNDS ALARM AS JPMORGAN CUTS PRIVATE CREDIT EXPOSURE
Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan, has begun reducing the bank’s $27 billion in loans to private lenders, a move that started in September. The early pullback signals tightening credit conditions and reinforces Dimon’s warnings about risks building in credit markets.
item.description:
DIMON SOUNDS ALARM AS JPMORGAN CUTS PRIVATE CREDIT EXPOSURE

Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan, has begun reducing the bank’s $27 billion in loans to private lenders, a move that started in September. The early pullback signals tightening credit conditions and reinforces Dimon’s warnings about risks building in credit markets.

item.title:
USO ETF NEARS 7-YEAR HIGH AS OIL SURGES
The U.S. Oil Fund (USO) is set to hit its highest price since 2018, rising over 40% this month amid Middle East tensions and oil nearing $100 a barrel, closing Thursday at $116.60.
item.description:
USO ETF NEARS 7-YEAR HIGH AS OIL SURGES

The U.S. Oil Fund (USO) is set to hit its highest price since 2018, rising over 40% this month amid Middle East tensions and oil nearing $100 a barrel, closing Thursday at $116.60.

item.description: IRAN SECURITY CHIEF SAYS WAR WON'T END SOON: WSJ

item.title:
DIESEL HITS $5+ IN 8 STATES, CALIFORNIA TOPS $6
GasBuddy reports that 8 U.S. states now have average diesel prices above $5 per gallon, with California soaring to $6.16. Other affected states include WA, WI, OR, PA, FL, VT, and ME.
item.description:
DIESEL HITS $5+ IN 8 STATES, CALIFORNIA TOPS $6

GasBuddy reports that 8 U.S. states now have average diesel prices above $5 per gallon, with California soaring to $6.16. Other affected states include WA, WI, OR, PA, FL, VT, and ME.

item.title:
SPR OIL RELEASE COULD TAKE WEEKS TO RELIEVE SHORTAGES
Emergency oil from the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve and other stockpiles could take weeks to reach markets, especially in Asia. Even after release, shipping delays mean oil may not arrive until mid-May, and the maximum daily flow covers less than 10% of the current supply disruption from the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description:
SPR OIL RELEASE COULD TAKE WEEKS TO RELIEVE SHORTAGES

Emergency oil from the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve and other stockpiles could take weeks to reach markets, especially in Asia. Even after release, shipping delays mean oil may not arrive until mid-May, and the maximum daily flow covers less than 10% of the current supply disruption from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title:
NEARLY HALF OF AMERICANS BLAME TRUMP FOR SURGING GAS PRICES
A Morning Consult poll shows 48% of Americans hold President Trump responsible for rising gas prices, higher than any other factor. Gas has jumped over 20% in the past month, averaging $3.60 per gallon, driven by global tensions and the Iran conflict.
While the administration calls the spike “short-term,” public frustration is mounting as gas costs hit an 11-day rise streak. Only 16% blame oil companies, 13% global markets, and 11% cite former President Biden.
item.description:
NEARLY HALF OF AMERICANS BLAME TRUMP FOR SURGING GAS PRICES

A Morning Consult poll shows 48% of Americans hold President Trump responsible for rising gas prices, higher than any other factor. Gas has jumped over 20% in the past month, averaging $3.60 per gallon, driven by global tensions and the Iran conflict.

While the administration calls the spike “short-term,” public frustration is mounting as gas costs hit an 11-day rise streak. Only 16% blame oil companies, 13% global markets, and 11% cite former President Biden.

item.title: Lloyds, Bank of Scotland and Halifax apps showed customers other users' transactions
item.description: The Lloyds Banking Group customers reported being able to view payments and charges from other sources.

item.title:
IRAN'S TOP SECURITY OFFICIAL
Iran’s Ali Larijani told Donald Trump that a quick victory is impossible, warning Washington it will face consequences for starting the conflict. “Starting a war is easy, but it cannot be won with a few tweets. We will not relent,” he said on X.
item.description:
IRAN'S TOP SECURITY OFFICIAL

Iran’s Ali Larijani told Donald Trump that a quick victory is impossible, warning Washington it will face consequences for starting the conflict. “Starting a war is easy, but it cannot be won with a few tweets. We will not relent,” he said on X.

item.title: What on earth is going on with the oil price?
item.description: Oil price moves have made headlines since the Iran conflict started - but why have there been such sharp swings?

item.title: 🚨 IRAN'S TOP SECURITY OFFICIAL LARIJANI IN RESPONSE TO TRUMP: 'WE WILL NOT RELENT UNTIL MAKING YOU SORRY FOR THIS GRAVE MISCALCULATION'
item.description: 🚨 IRAN'S TOP SECURITY OFFICIAL LARIJANI IN RESPONSE TO TRUMP: 'WE WILL NOT RELENT UNTIL MAKING YOU SORRY FOR THIS GRAVE MISCALCULATION'

item.title: *IRAN'S TOP SECURITY OFFICIAL TO TRUMP: WE WILL NOT RELENT
item.description: *IRAN'S TOP SECURITY OFFICIAL TO TRUMP: WE WILL NOT RELENT

item.title:
COOPERMAN: MARKETS TOO HIGH AMID GEOPOLITICAL RISKS
Billionaire investor Leon Cooperman warns that stocks are overpriced and vulnerable to a correction, citing geopolitical tensions and weak U.S. leadership. He points to risks from Iran, rising volatility, and potential stagflation or recession, noting the S&P 500 trades at its highest earnings multiple since 2020.
“Good news is treated with a yawn; bad news can send stocks down 15–20%,” he said, emphasizing that markets aren’t pricing in global uncertainty properly.
item.description:
COOPERMAN: MARKETS TOO HIGH AMID GEOPOLITICAL RISKS

Billionaire investor Leon Cooperman warns that stocks are overpriced and vulnerable to a correction, citing geopolitical tensions and weak U.S. leadership. He points to risks from Iran, rising volatility, and potential stagflation or recession, noting the S&P 500 trades at its highest earnings multiple since 2020.

“Good news is treated with a yawn; bad news can send stocks down 15–20%,” he said, emphasizing that markets aren’t pricing in global uncertainty properly.

item.title: BESSENT TO TRAVEL TO FRANCE MARCH 15-16 TO MEET WITH CHINESE VICE PREMIER HE LIFENG
item.description: BESSENT TO TRAVEL TO FRANCE MARCH 15-16 TO MEET WITH CHINESE VICE PREMIER HE LIFENG

item.title:
U.S. HEATING OIL HITS $5+ — TIGHT OIL SUPPLY BLOWS PRICES UP
U.S. residential heating oil prices have climbed above $5 a gallon — a level not seen since late 2022 — as the ongoing conflict involving Iran disrupts global crude and diesel flows through key routes like the Strait of Hormuz. This squeeze on supply has pushed energy markets higher and fed through into heating fuel costs.
item.description:
U.S. HEATING OIL HITS $5+ — TIGHT OIL SUPPLY BLOWS PRICES UP

U.S. residential heating oil prices have climbed above $5 a gallon — a level not seen since late 2022 — as the ongoing conflict involving Iran disrupts global crude and diesel flows through key routes like the Strait of Hormuz. This squeeze on supply has pushed energy markets higher and fed through into heating fuel costs.

item.title: UK troops at Iraq base shoot down Iranian drones, Healey says
item.description: British forces based in Iraq shot down two drones overnight, Defence Secretary John Healey has confirmed.

item.title: PM says Mandelson appointment was mistake as No 10 denies cover-up
item.description: Documents show Sir Keir Starmer was warned the peer's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein posed a "reputational risk".

item.title:
TRADERS DOUBT FED WILL CUT RATES IN 2026
Bond markets are scaling back expectations for a 2026 Fed rate cut, now pricing in just 24 basis points, down from 30, less than a quarter-point.
Yields on 2-year Treasuries rose to ~3.70% amid Middle East tensions, rising energy prices, and heavy corporate and Treasury bond supply, weakening demand for new issues.
item.description:
TRADERS DOUBT FED WILL CUT RATES IN 2026

Bond markets are scaling back expectations for a 2026 Fed rate cut, now pricing in just 24 basis points, down from 30, less than a quarter-point.

Yields on 2-year Treasuries rose to ~3.70% amid Middle East tensions, rising energy prices, and heavy corporate and Treasury bond supply, weakening demand for new issues.

item.title:
UK WARNS IRAN LIKELY LAYING MINES IN HORMUZ
John Healey says Iran is likely deploying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, defying U.S. warnings.
He warns this raises risks for commercial and naval ships, and that mine clearance is nearly impossible during conflict. The safest way to reopen the route, Healey says, is de-escalation.
item.description:
UK WARNS IRAN LIKELY LAYING MINES IN HORMUZ

John Healey says Iran is likely deploying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, defying U.S. warnings.

He warns this raises risks for commercial and naval ships, and that mine clearance is nearly impossible during conflict. The safest way to reopen the route, Healey says, is de-escalation.

item.title: Woman who kept vulnerable victim as slave sentenced to 13 years
item.description: Mandy Wixon, 56, forced the victim to clean her squalid home and subjected her to a miserable life.

item.title:
UK SAYS IRAN MAY BE MINING HORMUZ — DIFFERING FROM U.S. CLAIMS
The John Healey said UK intelligence suggests Iran may have begun laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route, raising maritime security concerns.
This contrasts with statements from the U.S. that it has no confirmed reports of mines being laid and has targeted suspected Iranian mine-laying vessels.
item.description:
UK SAYS IRAN MAY BE MINING HORMUZ — DIFFERING FROM U.S. CLAIMS

The John Healey said UK intelligence suggests Iran may have begun laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route, raising maritime security concerns.

This contrasts with statements from the U.S. that it has no confirmed reports of mines being laid and has targeted suspected Iranian mine-laying vessels.

*Walter Bloomberg (@DeItaone)

UK'S HEALEY SAYS IRAN IS LIKELY MINING HORMUZ STRAIT

HEALEY: EVIDENCE MOUNTS THAT IRAN MAY'VE STARTED MINING HORMUZ

— https://nitter.net/DeItaone/status/2032111537393963071#m

item.description: 🚨 *TRADERS NO LONGER FULLY PRICE IN ONE FED RATE CUT IN 2026

item.title:
IEA OIL RELEASE LIMITED BY TIMING, MIX
The International Energy Agency plans a 400 million-barrel emergency release to offset Strait of Hormuz disruptions, enough for roughly 32–45 days of supply if delivered quickly.
Analyst Homayoun Falakshahi warns impact depends on timing and logistics, and stocks include both crude and refined products, not all immediately refinery-ready.
item.description:
IEA OIL RELEASE LIMITED BY TIMING, MIX

The International Energy Agency plans a 400 million-barrel emergency release to offset Strait of Hormuz disruptions, enough for roughly 32–45 days of supply if delivered quickly.

Analyst Homayoun Falakshahi warns impact depends on timing and logistics, and stocks include both crude and refined products, not all immediately refinery-ready.

item.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

item.description: 🚨 TRUMP ADMINISTRATION SET TO SUSPEND JONES ACT TO TAME OIL PRICE

item.title:
US WAR WITH IRAN COSTS $11.3B IN FIRST WEEK
United States officials say the first six days of the conflict with Iran cost $11.3 billion, nearly $1.9 billion per day, not including ongoing ship operations and personnel costs.
The campaign has deployed two aircraft carriers, struck 5,000+ targets, and fired hundreds of interceptors against drones and missiles, with seven U.S. servicemembers killed.
Defense officials expect up to $50 billion more may be requested to replenish munitions, as lawmakers push for full accounting of the war’s financial and human toll.
item.description:
US WAR WITH IRAN COSTS $11.3B IN FIRST WEEK

United States officials say the first six days of the conflict with Iran cost $11.3 billion, nearly $1.9 billion per day, not including ongoing ship operations and personnel costs.

The campaign has deployed two aircraft carriers, struck 5,000+ targets, and fired hundreds of interceptors against drones and missiles, with seven U.S. servicemembers killed.

Defense officials expect up to $50 billion more may be requested to replenish munitions, as lawmakers push for full accounting of the war’s financial and human toll.

item.title: ‘Sitting ducks’: Oil tankers trapped in Gulf as Iran widens attacks on shipping
item.description: Tanker operators face growing risks whether they stay or flee through Strait of Hormuz
